title: Lantana odorata
intro: Lantana odorata, commonly known as Fragrant Lantana or Yellow Lantana, is a tropical shrub native to Central and South America, characterized by its clusters of small, fragrant yellow flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, offering potential health benefits such as wound healing, pain relief, and treatment of respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Lantana odorata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Lantana odorata is classified as a flowering plant in the family Verbenaceae. It belongs to the genus Lantana and is commonly known as yellow shrub or wild orange. The species Lantana odorata is a tropical plant native to Central and South America. It is closely related to other species in the Lantana genus, which includes over 150 species of flowering plants. Lantana odorata is also sometimes classified as Lantana camara var. odorata, indicating its close relationship with the more widely cultivated Lantana camara.
uses: Lantana odorata, also known as shrub lantana, is a species of flowering plant traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. In folk medicine, the plant is used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections. Its leaves and flowers are often used to make teas and infusions that are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is also said to have antiviral properties, making it useful in treating viral infections such as the common cold. However, further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Lantana odorata in treating various medical conditions.
benefits: Lantana odorata is known to have various health benefits, with its flowers being used to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory issues due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves have been traditionally used to treat digestive problems, including diarrhea and stomach ulcers. Its essential oil has been found to exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it effective against certain infections. The plant also contains flavonoids and other compounds that may help to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. Additionally, Lantana odorata has been used in traditional medicine to treat various skin conditions, including acne and eczema.

parts: Lantana odorata is a plant species known for its various medicinal properties, and the parts used for medicinal purposes include the leaves, flowers, and roots. preparations: Lantana odorata is used in herbal preparations to treat various health issues. The plant's leaves and flowers are typically dried and then used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions. side_effects: Lantana odorata is known to cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals due to its sap and leaves. Ingesting any part of the plant can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. The plant's volatile oils can also cause respiratory problems, such as coughing and shortness of breath, if inhaled in large quantities. Prolonged exposure to the plant's fragrance can cause headaches and dizziness in sensitive individuals. People with liver or kidney issues should avoid handling Lantana odorata, as it may exacerbate these conditions.
